name: Thyroidectomy

figure_scopes.overview: Thyroid, trachea, and strap-retracted neck anatomy
figure_scopes.storyboard: Overview -> superior pole vessels -> RLN & parathyroid preservation -> safe lobe mobilization
figure_scopes.closeup: Recurrent laryngeal nerve in the tracheoesophageal groove with parathyroids preserved

laterality: Anterior neck; recurrent laryngeal nerve ascends in the tracheoesophageal groove, parathyroid glands lie posterior to the thyroid
must_have: Thyroid lobe, trachea, recurrent laryngeal nerve, superior thyroid vessels, inferior thyroid artery, parathyroid glands, and Berry ligament region.
Posterior thyroid surface relationship for the parathyroid glands.
Recurrent laryngeal nerve course near the tracheoesophageal groove and Berry ligament.
spatial_rules: Recurrent laryngeal nerve should ascend in the tracheoesophageal groove region toward the larynx.
Parathyroid glands are small and posterior to the thyroid, not large anterior nodules.
Superior thyroid vessels are superior pole structures; the inferior thyroid artery has a posterior/lateral relationship.

forbidden_errors.error: Recurrent laryngeal nerve confused with the inferior thyroid artery.
Parathyroid glands placed on the anterior free surface as large masses.
Nerve omitted when showing Berry ligament or posterior dissection anatomy.

safety_concept: Preserve the recurrent laryngeal nerve and parathyroid glands during lobe mobilization.
phases: Recurrent laryngeal nerve and parathyroid preservation view.
Safe lobe mobilization with nerve/parathyroid preserved.
verify: Recurrent laryngeal nerve course is plausible and protected.
Parathyroid glands are posterior and small.
Superior and inferior vascular relationships are not swapped.
